ddls:
CREATE TABLE "election_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each election result */,
  "fips" INTEGER /* FIPS code for the state and county */,
  "state" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  "county" TEXT /* Name of the county */,
  "votes" INTEGER /* Total number of votes */,
  "absentee_votes" INTEGER /* Number of absentee votes */,
  "reporting" INTEGER /* Reporting percentage */,
  "precincts" INTEGER /* Number of precincts */,
  "absentee_method" TEXT /* Method used for absentee voting */,
  "election_date" TEXT /* Date of the election */,
  "election_type" TEXT /* Type of election (e.g., primary, general) */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number for dataset versioning */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "candidates" (
  "candidate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each candidate */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the candidate */,
  "party" TEXT /* Party affiliation of the candidate */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the candidate */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the candidate */,
  "incumbent" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the candidate is an incumbent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("candidate_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "candidate_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the election result */,
  "candidate_id" INTEGER /* ID of the candidate */,
  "votes" INTEGER /* Number of votes received by the candidate */,
  "percentage" REAL /* Percentage of votes received by the candidate */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the candidate in the election results */,
  "runoff_eligible" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the candidate is eligible for a runoff */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id", "candidate_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "absentee_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the election result */,
  "candidate_id" INTEGER /* ID of the candidate */,
  "absentee_votes" INTEGER /* Number of absentee votes received by the candidate */,
  "percentage" REAL /* Percentage of absentee votes received by the candidate */,
  "provisional_votes" INTEGER /* Number of provisional absentee votes received by the candidate */,
  "provisional_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of provisional absentee votes received by the candidate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id", "candidate_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  "abbreviation" TEXT /* Abbreviation for the state */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the state */,
  "voting_method" TEXT /* Primary voting method used in the state (e.g., mail, in-person) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "counties" (
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each county */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the county */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* ID of the state the county belongs to */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the county */,
  "voting_method" TEXT /* Primary voting method used in the county (e.g., mail, in-person) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("county_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_counties_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hash of the user's password for security */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the election result accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user who accessed the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_id") REFERENCES election_results ("result_id")
)
